spec.goal: Recover XY-156 on top of the latest mainline so Maestro can resume self-bootstrap work on repo-configurable global and per-state concurrency limits.
success_criteria: WORKFLOW.md and workflow contract docs define optional global and per-state concurrency settings with canonical defaults.
Workflow parsing exposes validated concurrency fields without regressing the latest canonical-only parser behavior from main.
StateStore and orchestrator logic on the recovered lane enforce global and per-state concurrency limits for dispatch, retry, and reconciliation.
The recovered lane passes repo-native verification and is ready to re-enter the normal review flow.
The stale retained XY-156 lane is no longer the execution authority for this issue.
constraints: Base all recovery work on origin/main at adf76d819cf80d6aa4be175227eb968e760b0de8 or a descendant.
Do not overwrite or reset the retained donor lane until the recovered lane has absorbed the useful implementation.
Keep WORKFLOW.md frontmatter TOML-based and preserve default single-slot behavior when concurrency fields are unset.
Do not widen scope into distributed scheduling or tracker contract changes unrelated to concurrency policy.
Use repo-native verification commands from cargo-make before claiming recovery is complete.

tasks.title: Recover workflow contract surface on latest main
tasks.objective: Port the donor lane changes for WORKFLOW.md, workflow docs, and workflow.rs onto the latest mainline while preserving the current canonical-only contract.
inputs: Retained donor lane diff for WORKFLOW.md, docs/spec/system_workflow_contract.md, and src/workflow.rs
Latest mainline parser behavior after XY-165 and XY-168
outputs: Updated WORKFLOW.md example with concurrency settings
WorkflowExecution parsing and validation for concurrency settings
verification: Targeted workflow parsing tests cover defaults and configured overrides
No legacy alias or compatibility guidance is reintroduced

tasks.title: Recover state and orchestrator concurrency logic
tasks.objective: Port and adapt the donor lane changes in state.rs and orchestrator.rs so the latest runtime enforces global and per-state concurrency limits consistently.
inputs: Retained donor lane diff for src/state.rs and src/orchestrator.rs
Recovered workflow execution surface from the prior task
outputs: StateStore lease/state updates needed for multi-lease concurrency accounting
Orchestrator gating for dispatch, retry, and reconciliation via shared concurrency checks
verification: Targeted state/orchestrator tests cover default single-slot behavior and configured concurrency

tasks.title: Verify recovered lane and capture recovery evidence
tasks.objective: Run repo-native checks on the recovered implementation and record the surviving lane as the new execution authority for XY-156.
outputs: Passing fmt, lint, and test evidence on the recovered lane
Updated plan state/evidence showing the surviving lane is review-ready

tasks.title: Recover daemon multi-child orchestration on latest main
tasks.objective: Replace the daemon's single active-child slot with orchestration that can hold multiple concurrent child runs up to the configured concurrency limits while preserving reconciliation and retry semantics.
inputs: Current daemon tick, child inspection, and retry scheduling logic in src/orchestrator.rs
outputs: Daemon tick flow that can track multiple active children
Spawn, reconciliation, and cleanup logic that respects configured global and per-state concurrency limits
verification: Targeted daemon/orchestrator tests cover multi-child spawning and cleanup behavior
Existing retry and reconciliation tests still pass

triggers: The donor patch no longer applies cleanly to the latest mainline in a way that preserves intended semantics.
Latest mainline changes require a different task split or different concurrency policy semantics.
Verification exposes regressions that cannot be fixed without reshaping the task graph.

evidence: Recovered WORKFLOW.md, docs/spec/system_workflow_contract.md, and src/workflow.rs on top of adf76d8 without reintroducing legacy project alias support.
Recovered src/state.rs and src/orchestrator.rs on top of adf76d8 with ConcurrencySnapshot gating for dispatch, retry blocking, run targeting, and recovered lease hydration.
Verification: cargo test candidate_selection_ -- --nocapture (7 passed, including multi-slot and per-state concurrency coverage).
Verification: cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, and cargo make test all passed on the surviving recovery lane.
Live dry-run was not rerun from the recovery lane because tmp/maestro.toml is not tracked in clone-backed workspaces; root local operator config remains intentionally untouched.
Review-prepare finding: daemon orchestration still tracked a single active_child slot, so configured concurrency above one was not yet realized in daemon mode.
Recovered daemon orchestration to track multiple active children and keep the parent dispatch guard while child processes inherit clones of the same project-level lock.
Verification: cargo test exited_retry_child_keeps_queued_claim_when_no_run_attempt_was_persisted -- --nocapture passed after the multi-child daemon refactor.
Verification: cargo make lint and cargo make test passed again after the daemon child-pool changes.

context_snapshot.recovery_reason: The retained XY-156 lane plan is invalid under the current plan/1 contract and the donor lane is based on a stale pre-XY-165/XY-168 mainline.
